title: Ethics & Compliance faculty (COI/open-meeting/records/gift/resource-separation), informational
context: The Ethics & Compliance faculty is the second core HIGH-tier faculty. It surfaces conflict-of-interest, open-meeting/sunshine, public-records, gift, campaign-vs-official-resource separation, and records-retention rules; flags when a contemplated action may violate them. It must be strictly informational (routes to the ethics board/counsel; never gives a verdict) and must refuse any request to help evade, delay, or minimize a transparency or ethics obligation -- redirecting to the lawful process instead. Every rule must be cited to primary law with expert sign-off before content ships.
objective: Implement the Ethics & Compliance faculty behind the guardrail layer, surfacing COI/open-meeting/records/gift/resource-separation/records-retention rules with source citations, flagging potential violations, refusing evasion requests with correct cited lawful-process redirects, and labeled informational not legal advice.
acceptanceCriteria: Faculty is mounted behind the guardrail layer (task 003)
Surfaces COI, open-meeting/sunshine, public-records, gift, campaign-vs-official-resource separation, and records-retention rules; flags when a contemplated action may cross one
Every surfaced rule carries a primary-source citation (citation-coverage test passes)
Refuses to help evade, delay, or minimize any transparency or ethics obligation; refusals include a correct, cited lawful-process pointer
All output labeled informational, not legal advice; routes to ethics board/counsel for binding determinations
Expert (ethics/legal) sign-off required before any real jurisdiction content is enabled through this faculty
CI green including red-team assertions on evasion-category refusals
output: A merged PR implementing the Ethics & Compliance faculty behind the guardrail layer, with source-cited rule surfacing, violation flagging, evasion refusals with cited lawful-process redirects, and informational labeling.
